expected_behavior: Scores 6 complexity criteria: correctness, maintainability, performance, security, scalability, testability
Uses 7-criterion scoring with 1-5 scale per criterion
Produces weighted composite score (0-10) from all dimension ratings
Limits scope to MAX 30 files to prevent context exhaustion
Decomposes architecture into individually assessable components and boundaries
Identifies risk factors and complexity hotspots
Provides pros/cons analysis with supporting evidence from the codebase
Compares current approach against alternatives with scores

evaluations.query: Assess the performance of our database queries
expected_behavior: Scores complexity criteria focused on estimation and risk for database analysis
Spawns agent for deep criterion-level scoring and assessment of task complexity
Includes effort estimation for each recommended improvement action
Prioritizes quick wins (effort <= 2, impact >= 4)
Answers 'is this good?' with structured decomposition and risk assessment
Produces overall score with grade interpretation (A+ through F)
Generates actionable improvement suggestions with effort/impact
Stores assessment results in memory for trend tracking

evaluations.query: Assess the quality of backend/app/services/payment_processor.py. This file is critical to review.
expected_behavior: Attempts to read the specified file and reports that it does not exist at the given path
Does not fabricate a code quality assessment for a file that cannot be read
Suggests searching for the file with alternative paths or similar names using Glob
Handles the file-not-found condition gracefully without producing a score

evaluations.query: Assess every single file in this monorepo. I want quality scores for all 5,000 files.
expected_behavior: Enforces the MAX 30 files scope limit to prevent context exhaustion
Explains that assessing 5,000 files exceeds the practical scope and would produce shallow results
Recommends focusing on high-risk modules, recently changed files, or known hotspots
Offers to prioritize which directories or components to assess first

evaluations.query: Assess the quality of this minified JavaScript file: vendor.min.js. It's 50,000 characters on one line.
expected_behavior: Identifies that minified or obfuscated code cannot be meaningfully assessed for code quality
Explains that readability, maintainability, and documentation scores are inapplicable to minified code
Recommends assessing the source files before minification instead of the build output
Does not produce misleading low scores that reflect the minification rather than actual code quality
